close_reason: keyring::Error::Ambiguous mapped to Backend is correct: ambiguity is a permanent config error
description:
On macOS, the Keychain may contain multiple credentials matching the same (service, account) tuple — keyring 3 returns Error::Ambiguous(Vec<Credential>). lib.rs:118 catches this in the catch-all arm and returns SecretError::Backend with a stringified error.

Impact: a user with a duplicate keychain item gets a generic 'backend desktop error: Entry is matched by N credentials' message and no clear path to recovery. The duplicate may have been created by another app (a common situation on macOS).

Recommend: leave the mapping as-is, but mention in module docs that ambiguous-match returns Backend on macOS, and link to the keyring crate's Ambiguous variant docs. This is a 'document the edge case' finding, not a code change.

(A proper fix — exposing 'pick first' semantics or surfacing the duplicates to the caller — would require a new SecretError variant or store API; out of scope.)
title: keyring::Error::Ambiguous mapped to Backend with no docs guidance
